name: Cledara SaaS Management and Spend API
what_it_does: SaaS management platform with virtual card payment infrastructure and API — providing visibility into SaaS subscriptions, automating spend control via virtual cards issued per software vendor, tracking renewals, managing software access, and providing finance-team-friendly spend analytics for growing tech companies.
use_cases: Querying SaaS subscription inventory and monthly spend via Cledara API for finance reporting
Integrating Cledara virtual card data with accounting systems (Xero, QuickBooks) for reconciliation
Monitoring SaaS renewal alerts and upcoming spend commitments via API
Managing employee software access approvals and requests via Cledara workflow integration
Pulling spend analytics for SaaS optimization and budget planning
not_for: Enterprise SaaS procurement with vendor negotiation (use Vendr or Sastrify for negotiation services)
Feature-level SaaS usage analytics (use Productiv for deep usage data)
Cloud IaaS spend management (use Vantage or CloudHealth for AWS/GCP/Azure)
best_when: A fast-growing tech startup (50-500 employees) needs visibility and control over SaaS spend via virtual cards that limit each vendor's charges automatically — giving finance teams control without slowing down software procurement.
avoid_when: You're an enterprise needing dedicated vendor negotiation services, or you need deep usage analytics rather than spend/access control.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow. Bearer token auth. Organization-scoped with role-based access (admin, finance, manager). Webhook for subscription events, renewal alerts, and card transactions. Accounting integrations (Xero, QuickBooks, Sage) via native connectors. HRIS integration for employee lifecycle. Scoped by resource type (subscriptions, cards, spending).

pricing.notes: London-based company. Founded 2019. Raised $14M Series A (2022). Backed by Notion Capital, Anthemis Group. Virtual card infrastructure via Modulr (UK) and Stripe (US). UK fintech regulator (FCA) authorized as payment institution. European-first focus — strong UK/EU compliance. Virtual card per vendor is the key differentiator — automatic spend control at payment layer.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per plan; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for subscriptions, virtual cards, transactions, and analytics. OAuth2 bearer auth. Good but limited public documentation. Virtual card management requires FCA-regulated payment operations — not all actions available via API. Accounting integration handles most finance workflow. Webhook for spend events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Subscription reads fully idempotent. Transaction reads idempotent. Card reads idempotent. Analytics reads idempotent by period.
known_agent_gotchas: Virtual card operations require FCA-regulated processes — not all card actions available via API
UK/EU focus — US organizations may find accounting integrations limited
Business bank account required for Cledara card funding
PSD2 compliance affects payment authentication flows in EU
Free tier limited to 10 subscriptions — insufficient for organizations with large SaaS portfolios

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, PSD2, SOC2 compliant. EU/UK data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. FCA-authorized payment institution — strong financial regulatory compliance. Virtual card data is financial PII — handle with payment card security standards. PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) for EU payments.
